PICNIC At MANITO.


The members and friends of the
Curcle No. of the ladies' aid of
the First Methodist Episcopal
church will hold their annual picnic
Thursday evening, July IA, at Man
ito park. A luncheon will be served
at 1 o'clock.


SUFFRAGE MEETING.


Attorney D. B. He'll and Mrs.
Philip P. Stafford will address the
Equal Suffrage meeting to be held
this evening in the Chamber of
Commerce. The musical numbers
will be given by Mrs. George Love
JOY as soloist and Miss Viola Kratz
at the piano.


& & Z
EMERALD CLUB WILL MEET.


Mrs. James +. Mama will be
hostess tomorrow afternoon when
she will entertain the members Of
the Emerald club at her residence
on Sixth avenue


{ & Z


LAWN FETE.
The home of Father De Kanter
will be the scene of brilliant af-
fair this evening when the men,
bers and friends of the st. Joseph's
Altar society will be entertained at
a lawn fete. A dainty luncheon
will be served.
4 4 Z
WILL ENTERTAIN CLUB.
Mrs. ]. w. Woodruff will enter
tain the members of the Progress
sive Needlework club Friday after
noon at her home. E1204 Montgom-
cry avenue.
OCCUPYING SUMNER HOME.
J. f. Sexton and family are &C.
cupying their summer home at
Loon Lake.
4 & =
CIRCLES WILL MEET.
Circle No. l of the First MethO-
dist church will hold it's regular
business meeting Thursday after
noon at 2:30 o'clock in Liberty
Park. On the same afternoon cir
Gle No. 2 will meet with Mrs. Bun
ton at her home, 1929 Fourteenth
avenue
